key_insight: A pretrained universal machine-learned interatomic potential can be fine-tuned to model oxygen plasma interactions with WS2 accurately enough for reactive molecular dynamics.
engineering_relevance: This provides a fast surrogate for DFT in plasma-surface simulations, enabling practical prediction of oxygen-induced surface chemistry and coverage on 2D materials.
method: Start from the pretrained UMA (uma-s-1p1) foundation model from OC20.
Iteratively sample diverse WS2-O plasma configurations using SOAP + farthest point sampling.
Label selected structures with PBE+D3+U+spin DFT and fine-tune on energy, force, and stress.
evidence_signal: The fine-tuned model reached MAE values of 4.5×10^-3 eV/atom for energy and 0.076 eV/angstrom for forces, while the pretrained model already matched chemisorbed S and O coverage under 15 eV O+ and O2+ bombardment.
watch_item: The best accuracy required spin-polarized DFT with Hubbard U during training, while the pretrained model’s successful coverage prediction without these physics may not generalize to all reactive plasma conditions.
paper_title: Fine-Tuning a Universal Machine-Learned Interatomic Potential for Oxygen Plasma Interactions with WS$_2$
